home.title: EcoKids India
home.subtitle: പഠിക്കൂ · കളിക്കൂ · ഭൂമി രക്ഷിക്കൂ
home.description: 1 മുതൽ 12 ക്ലാസ് വരെയുള്ള ആയിരക്കണക്കിന് വിദ്യാർഥികളോടൊപ്പം ചേർന്ന് ഇന്ററാക്ടീവ് ഗെയിമുകൾ, പരീക്ഷണങ്ങൾ, ക്വിസുകൾ വഴി പരിസ്ഥിതി സംരക്ഷണം പഠിക്കൂ.
home.mission_text: രസകരവും വിദ്യാഭ്യാസപരവുമായ രീതിയിൽ നമ്മുടെ ഭൂമിയുടെ പരിചരണവും സംരക്ഷണവും എങ്ങനെ ചെയ്യാം എന്ന് പഠിക്കൂ. റീസൈക്ലിങ്, ഊർജം, ജന്തുസംരക്ഷണം എന്നിവയെ കുറിച്ചുള്ള ഗെയിമുകൾ, പ്രവർത്തനങ്ങൾ, പാഠങ്ങൾ കണ്ടെത്തൂ.
home.games_subtitle: ഇന്ററാക്ടീവ് ഗെയിമുകളിലൂടെ പരിസ്ഥിതി ശാസ്ത്രത്തിന്റെ ലോകം ഒരുമിച്ച് പര്യവേക്ഷണം ചെയ്യുന്നതിൽ രസവും പഠനവും കൈകോർക്കുന്നു.
home.app_banner: ഫോണിലോ ടാബ്ലെറ്റിലോ നന്നായി കളിക്കൂ — ആപ്പ് സൗജന്യമായി ഡൗൺലോഡ് ചെയ്യൂ!

games.title: ഇക്കോ-ഗെയിമുകൾ
games.subtitle: കളിക്കൂ, പരിസ്ഥിതിയെ കുറിച്ച് അറിയൂ
games.lockedMessage: പഠിച്ചു കൊണ്ടേ ഇരിക്കൂ! ക്ലാസ് {{grade}} എത്തി ഈ ഗെയിം തുറക്കൂ.

experiments.title: ഇക്കോ-പരീക്ഷണങ്ങൾ
experiments.subtitle: ഓരോ ക്ലാസിനും പ്രായോഗിക ശാസ്ത്ര പ്രവർത്തനങ്ങൾ
experiments.submitPhotoDesc: നിങ്ങളുടെ പരീക്ഷണം പൂർത്തിയാക്കിയ ഫോട്ടോ എടുത്ത് EP നേടൂ!
experiments.dragDrop: വലിക്കൂ, ഉപേക്ഷിക്കൂ അല്ലെങ്കിൽ ക്ലിക്ക് ചെയ്ത് അപ്‌ലോഡ് ചെയ്യൂ

quiz.title: ഇക്കോ-ക്വിസുകൾ
quiz.subtitle: അറിവ് പരീക്ഷിക്കൂ, EP നേടൂ, ക്ലാസിൽ മുന്നിൽ നിൽക്കൂ

leaderboard.title: ലീഡർബോർഡ്
leaderboard.subtitle: ഭാരതത്തിലെ വിദ്യാർഥികളിൽ നിങ്ങൾ എവിടെ എന്ന് കാണൂ

profile.title: എന്റെ പ്രൊഫൈൽ
profile.name: പൂർണ്ണ നാമം
profile.noBadges: ഇനിയും ബാഡ്ജുകൾ ഇല്ല — പ്രവർത്തനങ്ങൾ പൂർത്തിയാക്കി നേടൂ!
profile.noActivity: ഇനിയും പ്രവർത്തനം ഇല്ല. കളിക്കാൻ ആരംഭിച്ച് ചരിത്രം കാണൂ!

auth.login_subtitle: നിന്റെ ഇക്കോ യാത്ര തുടരാൻ ലോഗിൻ ചെയ്യൂ
auth.signup_subtitle: സൗജന്യ അക്കൗണ്ട് ഉണ്ടാക്കി ഭൂമി രക്ഷിക്കാൻ ആരംഭിക്കൂ
auth.agreeTerms: സേവന നിബന്ധനകളും സ്വകാര്യതാ നയവും ഞാൻ സ്വീകരിക്കുന്നു

errors.unauthorized: ഈ പേജ് കാണാൻ ആദ്യം ലോഗിൻ ചെയ്യൂ.

empty.topics: വിഷയങ്ങൾ കണ്ടെത്തിയില്ല. ക്ലാസോ വിഭാഗമോ മാറ്റി നോക്കൂ.
empty.games: നിന്റെ ക്ലാസിന് ഇനിയും ഗെയിമുകൾ ഇല്ല. ഉടൻ വരും!
empty.experiments: ഈ ക്ലാസിന് ഇനിയും പരീക്ഷണങ്ങൾ ഇല്ല. ഉടൻ വരും!
empty.quizzes: ഈ ക്ലാസിന് ഇനിയും ക്വിസുകൾ ഇല്ല. ഉടൻ വരും!
empty.leaderboard: ഇനിയും റാങ്കിങ്ങ് ഇല്ല. പ്രവർത്തനങ്ങൾ പൂർത്തിയാക്കി ഇവിടെ വരൂ!
empty.badges: ഇനിയും ബാഡ്ജുകൾ ഇല്ല. ക്വിസും പരീക്ഷണവും ചെയ്ത് ബാഡ്ജ് നേടൂ!
empty.activity: ഇനിയും പ്രവർത്തനം ഇല്ല. അന്വേഷിക്കൂ, ചരിത്രം ഇവിടെ കാണൂ.

footer.description: ഇന്ററാക്ടീവ് പഠനത്തിലൂടെ ഭാരതത്തിലെ കുട്ടികളെ പരിസ്ഥിതി ചാമ്പ്യൻമാരാക്കൽ.
footer.newsletterDescription: ഇൻബോക്സിൽ ഏറ്റവും പുതിയ ഇക്കോ-ലേണിങ് ഉള്ളടക്കം ലഭിക്കൂ.

adventure.title: Eco Adventure
adventure.description: A grade-adaptive adventure from simple collection to advanced city climate simulation.
adventure.simulateCity: Simulation mode enabled for higher grades.
adventure.collectPrompt: Collect eco items and avoid pollutants.
adventure.quizPrompt: Answer grade-level questions and earn bonus EcoCoins.
adventure.curriculumPrompt: Aligned with NCERT and NEP 2020 themes.
adventure.higherMode: Higher grades manage city policy choices and a planet health meter.
adventure.defaultMode: Collect items, avoid hazards, and complete level checkpoints.
adventure.primaryRule: Primary: Emoji-first collection with simple visual play.
adventure.middleRule: Middle: Renewable icons and CO2 saved tracking.
adventure.seniorRule: Senior: Policy and data-informed level outcomes.

maze.title: Eco Maze
maze.hero: Find Clean Paths, Avoid Pollution Traps
maze.description: Navigate grade-wise mazes with checkpoints, knowledge gates, and policy decisions.
maze.subtext: Routes and outcomes adapt by grade from 4x4 beginner grids to dynamic policy mazes.
maze.primaryRule: Primary: 4x4 tap maze with large visual cells.
maze.middleRule: Middle/Senior: data zones, consequences, and route insights.
maze.higherRule: Higher: policy decision points reconfigure maze routes.
maze.reachCheckpoint: Reach a checkpoint to reveal vocabulary.
maze.policyDecision: Choose a policy to reshape your route.

connectDots.title: Eco Connect Dots
connectDots.description: From simple nature shapes to climate-system networks, connect ideas dot by dot.
connectDots.subtext: Node complexity scales by grade and each completion unlocks a curriculum-aligned fact.
connectDots.tapHint: Tap nodes in sequence to complete the pattern.

memory.title: Memory Match
memory.description: Flip cards and match environmental vocabulary adapted to each grade band.
memory.primaryRule: Primary: 4 pairs, emoji to simple word matching.
memory.juniorRule: Junior: English to Hindi vocabulary matching.
memory.seniorRule: Senior: Policy/law and science concept matching.
memory.higherRule: Higher: Advanced concepts and scientific explanations.

waste.title: Waste Sorting Game
waste.description: Sort waste correctly with grade-based bins, impact feedback, and policy strategy mode.
waste.subtext: Gameplay scales from emoji sorting to city-level sustainability planning.

Primary.0: Trees give us clean air to breathe 🌳
Primary.1: Water is precious - always turn off the tap! 💧
Primary.2: Plants need sunlight, water, and soil to grow 🌱
Primary.3: Birds eat insects and help our gardens 🐦
Primary.4: The Sun gives us light and warmth every day ☀️
Primary.6: Rain fills our rivers and lakes 🌧️
Primary.7: Cows, goats and hens are farm animals 🐄
Primary.8: We should not throw trash on the road 🚯
Primary.10: Sea turtles live in the ocean 🐢
Primary.11: Forests are homes for many animals 🦁
Primary.12: We get fruits and vegetables from plants 🥦
Primary.13: Butterflies help flowers grow by carrying pollen 🦋
Primary.14: Planting a tree is a gift to the earth 🌲

Junior.0: India has over 45,000 species of plants - one of the most diverse countries on Earth 🌍
Junior.1: The Ganga is India's longest river and provides water to over 500 million people 🏞️
Junior.2: Solar energy comes from the sun and does not pollute the air ☀️
Junior.3: Rainforests cover only 6% of Earth but are home to more than half of all species 🌿
Junior.4: Every year, India plants millions of trees on Van Mahotsav Day 🌳
Junior.5: Plastic takes over 400 years to break down in the environment 🚯
Junior.6: The Western Ghats are one of the world's 8 hottest hotspots of biodiversity 🦜
Junior.7: India gets about 70% of its rainfall during the monsoon season ☔
Junior.8: Bees pollinate one-third of our food crops and help farms grow 🐝
Junior.9: The Bengal Tiger is India's national animal and is endangered 🐯
Junior.10: Composting kitchen waste creates natural fertilizer for plants ♻️
Junior.11: Oceans cover 71% of Earth and make about half the oxygen we breathe 🌊
Junior.12: India has 18 Biosphere Reserves to protect nature 🏕️
Junior.13: Wind energy uses moving air to generate electricity with no pollution 💨
Junior.14: Deforestation can cause floods because trees hold soil and absorb rainwater 🌧️

Middle.0: India is home to 7-8% of all recorded species on Earth despite covering only 2.4% of the land area 🌏
Middle.1: The Sundarbans mangrove forest in India and Bangladesh is the largest in the world and home to Royal Bengal Tigers 🐯
Middle.2: India installed over 70 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2023 - fourth largest in the world ⚡
Middle.3: The Chipko Movement of 1973 in Uttarakhand was one of India's first major environmental protests 🌲
Middle.4: India generates about 26,000 tonnes of plastic waste every day and a large share remains uncollected 🗑️
Middle.5: The Great Indian Bustard in Rajasthan has fewer than 150 birds left in the wild 🦅
Middle.6: India's National Action Plan on Climate Change has eight missions including Solar, Water, and Green India 📋
Middle.7: One tree can absorb about 22 kg of carbon dioxide in a year and support breathable air 🌳
Middle.8: Himalayan glaciers are melting faster because of climate change ❄️
Middle.9: India is the third largest emitter of greenhouse gases, but its per-capita emissions are still low 📊
Middle.10: Project Tiger, launched in 1973, helped tiger numbers rise to over 3,000 by 2022 🐅
Middle.11: Urban heat islands make cities 2-3°C hotter than surrounding rural areas 🌡️
Middle.12: India's rivers support 1.4 billion people, but around 70% of surface water is polluted 💧
Middle.13: The Aravalli Range is one of the oldest mountain systems and helps stop desert spread 🏔️
Middle.14: Electronic waste contains toxic metals like lead and mercury that can poison soil and groundwater ⚠️

Senior.0: India committed to achieving Net Zero carbon emissions by 2070 at COP26 in Glasgow 🌐
Senior.1: The carbon cycle, nitrogen cycle, and water cycle are interconnected - disrupting one affects the others 🔄
Senior.2: India's climate targets aim for 50% electric power capacity from non-fossil sources by 2030 ⚡
Senior.3: Eutrophication happens when fertilizer runoff causes algae blooms that reduce oxygen in water 🔬
Senior.4: The Biological Diversity Act 2002 guides conservation, sustainable use, and benefit-sharing in India 📜
Senior.5: Per capita water availability in India has fallen sharply since 1951 📉
Senior.6: The Environment Protection Act 1986 was passed after the Bhopal Gas Tragedy ⚖️
Senior.7: Soil erosion makes India lose billions of tonnes of topsoil every year 🌍
Senior.8: Coral bleaching starts when sea temperatures rise just 1-2°C above normal 🪸
Senior.9: India's GRIHA system rates how sustainable a building is 🏢
Senior.10: Food systems contribute a large share of greenhouse gas emissions, so diet choices matter 🥗
Senior.11: Wetlands cover 4.6% of India's area and support thousands of species 🦢
Senior.12: The Paris Agreement seeks to limit warming close to 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels 🌡️
Senior.13: India is among the world leaders in installed wind energy capacity 💨
Senior.14: Phytoremediation uses plants to remove contaminants from polluted soil and water 🌱

Higher.0: The IPCC Sixth Assessment Report warns that warming beyond 1.5°C risks irreversible climate tipping points 📄
Higher.1: India's Perform, Achieve and Trade scheme has delivered major industrial energy savings since 2012 🏭
Higher.2: Ecosystem services such as pollination, water purification, and soil fertility have immense economic value 💰
Higher.3: India's Coastal Regulation Zone rules protect 7,516 km of coastline from unchecked development 🏖️
Higher.4: Carbon sequestration in forests, soils, and oceans helps remove carbon dioxide from the atmosphere 🌳
Higher.5: Green Hydrogen is produced using electrolysis powered by renewable energy 🔬
Higher.6: Planetary boundaries describe nine Earth-system limits for safe human development 🌏
Higher.7: India's National Solar Mission and the International Solar Alliance promote rapid solar expansion ☀️
Higher.8: A circular economy keeps materials in use through reuse, repair, remanufacture, and recycling ♻️
Higher.9: The Kunming-Montreal biodiversity framework aims to protect 30% of land and oceans by 2030 🌿
Higher.10: Life Cycle Assessment tracks a product's environmental footprint from extraction to disposal 📊
Higher.11: India's Extended Producer Responsibility rules make producers answerable for end-of-life waste 📦
Higher.12: Agroforestry can improve farm resilience, boost yield, and store more carbon in landscapes 🌾
Higher.13: Air quality remains one of India's biggest environmental performance challenges 📉
Higher.14: Environmental Impact Assessment is mandatory in India for many major projects before construction 🏗️

0.explanation: Trees help clean the air and give us oxygen.

1.prompt: What colour is a healthy leaf?
1.explanation: Most healthy leaves are green because they contain chlorophyll.

2.explanation: Bees carry pollen from flower to flower.

3.prompt: What should we do with trash?
3.explanation: Putting trash in a bin keeps roads and water clean.

4.explanation: Fish live in ponds, lakes, rivers, and oceans.

5.prompt: What does the sun give us?
5.explanation: The sun gives Earth light and heat.

6.prompt: Which is a way to save water?
6.explanation: Using less water helps save this precious resource.

7.prompt: What animal is India's national animal?
7.explanation: The Bengal Tiger is India's national animal.

8.prompt: What do plants need to grow?
8.explanation: Plants need sunlight, water, air, and soil nutrients.

9.explanation: Recycling turns used materials into something useful again.

10.explanation: Clean paper can usually be recycled.

11.explanation: Tulsi is a plant often grown at home in India.

12.explanation: Clouds bring rain that fills rivers and ponds.

13.prompt: Which place is home for many animals?
13.explanation: Forests provide food and shelter for wildlife.

14.prompt: What do we plant to help Earth?
14.explanation: Planting trees helps air, soil, and animals.

0.prompt: What is the national river of India?
0.explanation: The Ganga is recognised as India's national river.

1.prompt: How long does plastic take to decompose?
1.explanation: Plastic can stay in the environment for hundreds of years.

2.prompt: Which energy source comes from the sun?
2.explanation: Solar energy is captured from sunlight.

3.explanation: WWF works to protect nature and wildlife.

4.prompt: Which gas do plants absorb from air?
4.explanation: Plants take in carbon dioxide for photosynthesis.

5.prompt: What is the name for the layer protecting Earth from UV rays?
5.explanation: The ozone layer blocks much of the Sun's harmful UV radiation.

6.prompt: Where is the Sundarbans forest located?
6.explanation: The Sundarbans spread across West Bengal and Bangladesh.

7.prompt: Which of these is a renewable energy source?
7.explanation: Wind is a renewable source because nature keeps replacing it.

8.explanation: Cutting forests can increase floods, droughts, and soil loss.

9.explanation: The Indian Peacock is the national bird.

10.explanation: Kitchen peels break down into compost.

11.prompt: What do bees help make possible?

12.prompt: Which season brings most rain to India?
12.explanation: Most of India's annual rainfall comes during the monsoon.

13.prompt: Which place is famous for rich biodiversity in India?
13.explanation: The Western Ghats are a biodiversity hotspot.

14.prompt: What should you carry to avoid single-use plastic?
14.explanation: A cloth bag helps reduce plastic waste.

0.prompt: What percentage of Earth's surface is covered by oceans?
0.explanation: Oceans cover about 71% of Earth's surface.

1.prompt: India's Project Tiger was launched in which year?

2.prompt: Which Indian state has the highest forest cover area?
2.explanation: Madhya Pradesh has the largest forest area in India.

3.prompt: What is the main greenhouse gas discussed in climate studies?
3.explanation: Carbon dioxide is the most discussed greenhouse gas in school climate science.

4.prompt: The Chipko Movement was associated with saving?
4.explanation: Villagers hugged trees to stop logging.

5.prompt: India was ranked what in installed solar capacity globally in 2023?
5.explanation: India was among the top four countries in solar capacity.

options: Algae bloom due to excess nutrients
6.explanation: Nutrient-rich runoff can trigger algae blooms and oxygen loss.

7.explanation: Organic materials such as banana peels decompose naturally.

8.prompt: The Silent Valley National Park is in which state?
8.explanation: Silent Valley National Park is in Kerala.

9.prompt: How much of India's surface water is estimated to be polluted?
9.explanation: A large share of India's surface water is polluted and needs treatment.

10.prompt: What is an urban heat island?
options: A city that stays hotter than nearby rural areas
10.explanation: Concrete and fewer trees can trap heat in cities.

options: They protect coasts and support biodiversity
11.explanation: Mangroves protect shorelines and shelter many species.

12.explanation: Old phones, wires, and computers are e-waste.

13.prompt: Why do glaciers matter in India?
13.explanation: Himalayan glaciers feed many rivers used by millions of people.

14.prompt: Which range helps check desert expansion in north-west India?
14.explanation: The Aravalli Range acts as a natural barrier against desertification.

0.prompt: India's Net Zero target year is?
0.explanation: India announced a Net Zero target year of 2070.

1.prompt: The Environment Protection Act in India was passed in?

2.explanation: COP refers to the Conference of the Parties to the climate convention.

3.prompt: The Paris Agreement aims to limit warming to?
3.explanation: The 1.5°C goal guides global climate ambition.

4.prompt: What is the main cause of ozone depletion?
4.explanation: CFCs damage ozone molecules in the stratosphere.

5.prompt: Biological Diversity Act was passed in India in?
5.explanation: The Biological Diversity Act came into force in 2002.

6.prompt: What is the GRIHA rating system for?
6.explanation: GRIHA rates environmental performance of buildings.

7.prompt: Which sector contributes the largest share to India's greenhouse gas emissions?
7.explanation: The energy sector contributes the largest share.

8.explanation: EIA reviews environmental risks before projects proceed.

9.explanation: India's coastline is about 7,516 km long.

10.explanation: Fertilizer and sewage runoff add excess nutrients to water bodies.

11.prompt: Which law followed the Bhopal disaster and strengthened environmental governance?
11.explanation: The Environment Protection Act 1986 followed the Bhopal disaster.

12.prompt: What does per capita water availability measure?
12.explanation: It estimates how much water is available for each person.

options: It uses plants to remove contaminants
13.explanation: Some plants absorb pollutants from soil and water.

14.prompt: Which agreement guides global climate action after 2015?
14.explanation: The Paris Agreement is the major climate accord adopted in 2015.

0.prompt: What does the PAT scheme stand for?
0.explanation: PAT is a market-based energy efficiency scheme for industry.

1.prompt: India's climate target aims for what percentage of power capacity from non-fossil sources by 2030?
1.explanation: India's target is 50% power capacity from non-fossil sources.

2.prompt: Kunming-Montreal framework targets protecting what percentage of land and ocean by 2030?
2.explanation: The target is commonly called 30 by 30.

3.explanation: Electrolysis splits water using electricity from clean sources.

options: Total environmental impact from production to disposal
4.explanation: LCA evaluates impact across a product's full life cycle.

5.prompt: India's rank in Environmental Performance Index 2022 was?

6.prompt: Which Indian scheme saves energy through market mechanisms?
6.explanation: PAT rewards industries that overachieve energy-saving targets.

7.prompt: What is the estimated annual value of global ecosystem services?
7.explanation: Ecosystem services are often valued at around $125 trillion annually.

8.prompt: Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) holds who responsible?
8.explanation: EPR makes producers responsible for end-of-life waste management.

9.explanation: Trees on farms can improve soil, resilience, and carbon storage.

options: Nine Earth-system limits for safe human operation
10.explanation: Planetary boundaries define safe operating limits for humanity.

options: It keeps materials in use and reduces extraction
11.explanation: Circular systems reduce waste by designing for reuse and recovery.

12.explanation: Greenwashing makes products or companies seem more sustainable than they are.

13.prompt: Which document gives the latest scientific consensus on climate risks?
13.explanation: IPCC reports synthesise current climate science.

14.prompt: Why is EIA important before construction?
options: It checks environmental risks and mitigation
14.explanation: EIA helps identify and manage project impacts before approval.

0.meaning: How plants make food using sunlight

1.meaning: The total greenhouse gases caused by a person or activity

2.meaning: A gas that traps heat in the atmosphere

4.meaning: Algae growth caused by too many nutrients in water

5.meaning: Using resources in a way that lasts for the future

7.meaning: A resource that can be replaced naturally

8.meaning: The natural home of an organism

9.meaning: Something released into the environment, often gases

11.meaning: An area where water drains to the same river or lake

12.meaning: A large mass of slowly moving ice

13.meaning: A city area hotter than nearby rural land

0.meaning: Storage of carbon in forests, soils, or oceans

2.meaning: Oxygen loss in water caused by excess nutrients

3.meaning: Using plants to clean polluted soil or water

4.meaning: The zone of life on Earth

5.meaning: The UN body that assesses climate science

6.meaning: National climate targets under the Paris Agreement

7.meaning: Coastal Regulation Zone rules for India

10.meaning: Actions that reduce climate change causes

11.meaning: Changes that help societies cope with climate impacts

13.meaning: The upper fertile layer of soil

0.meaning: A proposed epoch shaped strongly by human activity

1.meaning: A threshold after which change becomes hard to reverse

2.meaning: An economy designed to eliminate waste and reuse materials

3.meaning: Balancing emissions released with emissions removed

4.meaning: Analysis of environmental impact from raw material to disposal

5.meaning: Nine limits that define a safe operating space for humanity

6.meaning: A tradeable unit representing one tonne of carbon reduced or removed
